SEARCH
Module overrungl.glfw
Package overrungl.glfw

Interface GLFWReallocateFun

All Superinterfaces:
overrun.marshal.Upcall
Functional Interface:
This is a functional interface and can therefore be used as the assignment target for a lambda expression or method reference.
@FunctionalInterface public interface GLFWReallocateFun extends overrun.marshal.Upcall
The function pointer type for memory reallocation callbacks.

This is the function pointer type for memory reallocation callbacks. A memory reallocation callback function has the following signature: 

MemorySegment functionName(MemorySegment block, long size, MemorySegment user);
Since:
0.1.0
Author:
squid233
See Also:
  • GLFWAllocator

  • Field Details

    • TYPE

      The type of the upcall.

  • Method Details

    • invoke

      MemorySegment invoke(MemorySegment block, long size, MemorySegment user)
      This function must return a memory block at least size bytes long, or MemorySegment.NULL if allocation failed. Note that not all parts of GLFW handle allocation failures gracefully yet.

      This function must support being called during GLFW.init() but before the library is flagged as initialized, as well as during GLFW.terminate() after the library is no longer flagged as initialized.

      Any memory allocated via this function will be deallocated via the same allocator during library termination or earlier.

      Any memory allocated via this function must be suitably aligned for any object type. If you are using C99 or earlier, this alignment is platform-dependent but will be the same as what MemoryUtil.realloc(java.lang.foreign.MemorySegment, long) provides. If you are using C11 or later, this is the value of alignof(max_align_t).

      The block address will never be NULL and the size will always be greater than zero. Reallocations of a block to size zero are converted into deallocations before reaching the custom allocator. Reallocations of NULL to a non-zero size are converted into regular allocations before reaching the custom allocator.

      If this function returns MemorySegment.NULL, GLFW will emit GLFW.OUT_OF_MEMORY.

      This function must not call any GLFW function.

      Parameters:
      block - The address of the memory block to reallocate.
      size - The new minimum size, in bytes, of the memory block.
      user - The user-defined pointer from the allocator.
      Returns:
      The address of the newly allocated or resized memory block, or MemorySegment.NULL if an error occurred.
      Thread safety:
      This function must support being called from any thread that calls GLFW functions.
      Reentrancy:
      This function should not call any GLFW function.
      Pointer lifetime:
      The returned memory block must be valid at least until it is deallocated.

    • invoke_int

      default MemorySegment invoke_int(MemorySegment block, int size, MemorySegment user)

      user - The user-defined pointer from the allocator.
      Returns:

    • invoke

      block - The address of the memory block to reallocate.
      size - The new minimum size, in bytes, of the memory block.
